message:
feat(atc): add user-facing surfaces — state taxonomy, noise control, safe defaults, and golden workflows (br-0qt6e.4.6)

New atc_user_surfaces.rs module implementing:
- SurfaceState: 9-state taxonomy (Observation, Recommendation, Executed,
  Suppressed, FairnessThrottle, DeliberateNoOp, EvidenceDistrust,
  AttributionConfounded, SafeToIgnore) with actionability/visibility rules
- SurfaceCard: atomic unit of ATC output with headline, evidence, risk,
  next action, trust level, and attribution clarity
- NOISE_POLICIES: per-surface-type noise control (8 policies covering
  advisories, probes, release warnings, learning alerts, etc.)
- SAFE_DEFAULTS: conservative surface defaults (summary view, 50% interrupt
  threshold, show distrust/confounded, hide no-ops)
- GOLDEN_WORKFLOWS: 8 canonical operator workflows defining expected surface
  behavior, expected actions, and anti-patterns
- EvidenceTrustLevel and AttributionClarity with user-facing explanations
- 23 passing unit tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"br-0qt6e.4.3","title":"ATC Learning 4.3: TUI, system health, and operator visibility for ATC learning state","description":"# ATC Learning 4.3: TUI, system health, and operator visibility for ATC learning state

## Why
Operators should be able to tell whether ATC is learning well, drifting, rolling back, wisely deciding not to act, or preparing a meaningful change whose likely consequences should be reviewed before approval or trust.

## Deliverables
- surface learning metrics in the relevant TUI, system-health, and operator panels
- show calibration stress, regret trend, regime transitions, open-experience backlog, current execution posture, and fairness or burden-concentration stress
- clearly separate observation, recommendation, executed intervention, suppressed intervention, safe mode, rollback, and preview-only states in the UI
- make deliberate no-op and safe-to-ignore situations legible so operators are not trained to chase harmless noise
- ensure severe ATC failures are visible and not silently buried in generic health noise
- provide drill-down paths from summary views to deeper evidence, preview artifacts, and realized-outcome comparisons needed for diagnosis
- surface freshness, last successful update time, degraded-data conditions, material change highlights, and stale-preview conditions so stale or partial ATC state cannot masquerade as healthy calm
- document where each learning metric, decision-state indicator, and preview-versus-realized signal lives in the operator surfaces

## Acceptance Criteria
- the plan explicitly requires operators to distinguish healthy calm, degraded learning, active intervention, safety-gated non-action, and preview-only pending change states from visible surfaces alone
- summary views stay low-noise and progressive, while deep views preserve full forensic detail and preview-versus-realized links
- severe failures, rollback conditions, unexplained intervention suppression, stale/degraded ATC state, or invalidated previews cannot be silently hidden behind generic green health status

## Important Notes
The goal is not more dashboards. The goal is reliable situational awareness with progressive disclosure and low operator Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.4.3","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.4.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:48:15.880776874Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-0qt6e.4.4","title":"ATC Learning 4.4: policy bundle snapshots, audit summaries, and archive integration","description":"# ATC Learning 4.4: Policy bundle snapshots, audit summaries, archive integration, and preview-versus-realized accountability

## Why
ATC needs durable explainability artifacts for policy changes and regime transitions, but raw experience history should not flood Git. The archive needs concise, discoverable audit objects that explain what changed, why it changed, what was expected to happen beforehand, how attribution confidence or confoundedness affected the story, and how to trace that story back to evidence and realized outcomes.

## Deliverables
- define periodic or event-driven policy bundle snapshots
- emit human-readable audit summaries for promotions, rollbacks, regime changes, material safety-gated non-actions, attribution-confounded outcomes that materially affect trust, and any important control change where the absence or restriction of action itself matters
- emit a compact machine-readable companion summary or diff for automation and cross-surface consistency checks
- integrate those artifacts into the existing archive surface without storing raw telemetry exhaust
- preserve traceability from a policy artifact back to the evidence, decision IDs, experience IDs, rollups, attribution-confidence/confoundedness state, preview artifacts, and relevant summary snapshot or surface state that justified it
- where relevant, link each audit object to the immediately previous comparable artifact, to any baseline or reference comparator, and to the associated preflight preview so the change is legible before-and-after
- record predicted-versus-realized comparisons for promoted policies, important operator control changes, or major interventions when that comparison materially affects trust
- record when later outcomes were judged too causally confounded to cleanly validate or invalidate the earlier preview/certificate, rather than forcing a fake verdict
- keep audit summaries concise by default while pointing to deeper evidence for forensic follow-up
- document the retention and discoverability story for these artifacts

## Acceptance Criteria
- a future operator can answer what changed, why it changed, what was predicted, what actually happened, whether attribution was clean enough to trust, what evidence supported it, what changed relative to the last relevant artifact, and what to inspect next without consulting chat history
- archive integration preserves auditability without turning Git into a write-amplified telemetry sink
- summaries explicitly support user-facing explanation needs rather than dumping internal math jargon into the archive
- machine-readable audit companions are aligned with the canonical summary, preview, and attribution-confidence contracts rather than inventing conflicting interpretations

## Important Notes
This bead is the durable accountability layer for adaptation. If it is weak, later learning gains will be difficult to  {"id":"br-0qt6e.4.5","title":"ATC Learning 4.5: explainability cards and operator controls for adaptation state","description":"# ATC Learning 4.5: Explainability cards, bounded operator controls, and preflight decision support

## Why
If ATC is going to adapt autonomously, operators need direct visibility into the reasoning surfaces, a way to constrain the system without patching code, and a truthful preview of likely consequences before they trust a control change, policy promotion, or other high-leverage adjustment.

## Deliverables
- define the minimal operator controls for safe mode, exploration budget, policy inspection, fairness or burden controls, and other bounded runtime constraints that do not bypass auditability
- define the explainability-card format for current policy, active regime, recent promotion or rollback decisions, important deliberate no-action or suppression decisions, causally-confounded outcomes that materially affect interpretation, and pending changes awaiting human trust or bounded approval
- require preflight impact previews for the operator changes or policy moves where advance visibility materially improves safety or trust
- make controls deterministic, audit-friendly, reversible where appropriate, and tied to explicit policy/evidence identifiers plus preview artifacts
- ensure controls interact safely with admissibility gates, fairness constraints, preview freshness rules, attribution-confidence/confoundedness rules, and rollback logic
- define how cards and controls link out to deeper audit artifacts, decision IDs, experience IDs, preview-versus-realized comparisons, attribution-confidence/confoundedness evidence, and surface-specific drill-downs
- document operator workflows for reading, previewing, constraining, and later validating ATC learning behavior, including when the correct interpretation is \"multiple causes overlap, do not trust a clean causal story yet\"

## Acceptance Criteria
- operator controls cannot silently circumvent the learning system safety envelope, fairness contract, preview-freshness contract, attribution-honesty contract, or traceability requirements
- explainability cards cover both action and non-action decisions that materially affect users/operators, including cases where the most useful output is \"do not trust this preview yet\" or \"the outcome happened, but attribution is still confounded\"
- future rollout and hardening work can inspect this bead to understand what operators are allowed to change, what preview they should see first, and what evidence they should review afterward

## Important Notes
This bead is about constrained agency for operators, not an ad hoc debug panel. The controls must make the system safer and more legible, not more chaotic.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T19:58:12.939906824Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-18T02:00:57.763102788Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","atc","atc-learning","atc-learning-surfaces"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.4.5","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.1.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T20:32:34.268663489Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.4.5","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:58:09.883778942Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.4.5","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:46:45.400877087Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.4.5","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3.14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T02:00:57.761989363Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.4.5","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T20:09:27.429505326Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.4.5","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T20:27:08.791180345Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.4.5","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T22:14:21.024397452Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.4.5","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T19:58:12.939906824Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.4.5","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T20:08:57.914080691Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.4.5","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T20:09:07.7277390 -{"id":"br-0qt6e.4.6","title":"ATC Learning 4.6: user-facing utility, remediation guidance, noise control, and safe defaults","description":"# ATC Learning 4.6: User-facing utility, remediation guidance, noise control, and safe defaults

## Why
Even a mathematically strong ATC system will feel bad if it is noisy, opaque, hard to act on, quietly unfair, or unable to say when the evidence itself is not trustworthy. This bead exists to optimize the system for real operators and agents without dropping any capability.

## Deliverables
- define what every major ATC surface should answer for the user: what happened, why it happened, how risky it is, what to do next, what can be safely ignored, whether the current evidence is trustworthy enough to lean on, and whether multiple overlapping causes mean the system cannot yet tell a clean causal story
- ensure robot, TUI, system-health, and audit-summary surfaces present actionable remediation guidance rather than raw metrics alone
- define noise-control policy for advisories, probes, repeated warnings, degraded-learning alerts, suspicious-evidence alerts, causally-confounded-outcome alerts, and repeated safe-no-action states so the system is informative without becoming spammy
- define fairness and anti-starvation expectations for the user-facing experience so the same agent/project/cohort is not repeatedly burdened or ignored without explanation
- define safe defaults for interruptive vs non-interruptive surfaces, escalation thresholds, repeat suppression, summary-vs-drill-down behavior, distrust messaging when evidence is quarantined or low quality, and honesty messaging when attribution is confounded rather than unknown
- define golden user workflows and explicit non-alert or safe-to-ignore cases so later implementation preserves calm operator ergonomics instead of optimizing only for internal completeness
- require surfaces to distinguish observation, recommendation, executed intervention, suppressed intervention, fairness throttle, deliberate no-op, robustness-driven distrust, and attribution-confounded states in terms users can actually understand
- preserve full forensic detail in deep views while keeping default views concise, trustworthy, low-noise, and fair in how they distribute attention and friction
- document user-facing success criteria so later implementation does not optimize only internal elegance while degrading operator experience

## Acceptance Criteria
- the plan explicitly protects actionability, calm, fairness, evidence-trust legibility, and attribution honesty: operators should know when to act, when to wait, when to ignore ATC output, when a cohort is being over-burdened or starved, when suspicious evidence means \"do not trust this yet\", and when overlapping causes mean \"the outcome is real but the credit story is not clean yet\"
- no major surface is allowed to present safety-gated non-action, fairness-driven suppression, contamination-driven distrust, or attribution-confounded outcomes as unexplained silence
- later implementation beads can derive concise defaults, escalation rules, distrust messaging, attribution-honesty messaging, fairness expectations, and deep-drill paths from this bead alone without needing chat archaeology

## Important Notes
This bead is about user value, not simplification. The goal is to preserve all ATC power while making the system safer, clearer, fairer, more causally honest, and more useful for the humans and agents who have to live with it.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T20:44:53.261639580Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-18T02:02:05.780589295Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","atc","atc-learning","atc-learning-surfaces"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.4.6","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.1.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T20:44:53.261639580Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.4.6","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.2.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T20:44:53.261639580Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.4.6","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:10:06.209279466Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.4.6","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:58:52.055199151Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.4.6","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:46:57.034574562Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.4.6","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3.14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T02:02:05.779219510Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.4.6","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.3.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T20:44:53.261639580Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.4.6","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T20:44:53.261639580Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-0qt6e.4.7","title":"ATC Learning 4.7: preflight impact previews for policy changes, operator controls, and pending interventions","description":"# ATC Learning 4.7: Preflight impact previews for policy changes, operator controls, and pending interventions

## Why
ATC becomes much more trustworthy if users can see the likely consequences of a policy promotion, runtime control change, or material pending intervention before it lands. Without a preflight preview contract, the system can still be technically auditable yet feel like it acts first and explains later.

## Deliverables
- define a preflight impact-preview artifact for pending policy promotions, operator control changes, and material pending interventions where advance visibility meaningfully changes trust or decision quality
- define the predicted deltas the preview must surface: action volume, false-action risk, operator-noise budget pressure, fairness or burden-concentration impact, backlog pressure, safety-gate stress, rollback likelihood, and any important no-action or suppression consequences
- define the uncertainty, support-quality, evidence-trust, regime-stability, causal-isolation, and attribution-confidence indicators that must accompany any preview so weak, suspicious, strategically contaminated, or interference-heavy evidence is visible before a user over-trusts it
- define machine-readable reason codes for why a preview is unavailable, degraded, stale, support-limited, contamination-limited, interference-limited, or intentionally refused
- define how preview artifacts link to the candidate policy or control change, current incumbent or baseline, relevant certificate or fairness state, attribution-confidence state, evidence-quality state, and later realized outcomes so prediction-versus-reality can be audited
- define how robot, TUI/system-health, explainability cards, and archive/audit artifacts should expose previews without regressing into noisy shadow-mode theater or expensive full-history scans
- define when a preview is mandatory before a runtime control change or policy promotion is allowed versus when it is merely informative
- define how preview freshness works so an old preview cannot be mistaken for a current commitment under a changed regime, evidence state, contamination state, or competing concurrent change that destroys causal comparability

## Acceptance Criteria
- the plan explicitly requires ATC to show likely user-facing consequences before major adaptive or operator-driven changes are trusted
- previews must expose uncertainty, weak support, stale evidence, suspicious evidence, interference-heavy causal ambiguity, and fairness or noise tradeoffs rather than presenting a false aura of precision
- downstream surfaces, tests, and rollout checks can compare predicted versus realized behavior from this bead alone without chat archaeology
- the preview contract is clearly framed as bounded preflight visibility, not a resurrection of public shadow mode or a license for expensive speculative simulation

## Important Notes
This bead is about preflight legibility and safer operator trust. It should make the system feel more predictable and accountable before action, not just after the  {"id":"br-0qt6e.5","title":"ATC Learning Phase 5: replay, verification, and hardening","description":"# Phase 5: replay, verification, hardening, and rollout trust gates

## Why This Exists
The ATC learning stack should not be considered complete because the math sounds good or the code compiles. Phase 5 exists to prove, with reproducible evidence, that the system behaves correctly, stays performant, emits useful diagnostics, preserves user trust across acted and non-acted paths, and tells the truth about both realized outcomes and preflight predictions.

## Scope
- deterministic scenario corpus and replay harnesses
- comprehensive unit/property tests with rich diagnostics and cross-surface consistency checks
- detailed E2E scripts with artifact-rich logging
- performance, storage, contention, and optimization passes grounded in scenario-derived workloads
- forensic artifact standards, remote verification, baseline comparisons, and final rollout sign-off
- predicted-versus-realized checks for impact previews, policy changes, and operator control workflows

## Acceptance Criteria
- this phase explicitly requires comprehensive unit tests, detailed E2E scripts, and evidence-rich logging instead of smoke-test theater
- verification covers acted, suppressed, deliberate no-action, stale/degraded, fairness-stressed, and preview-versus-realized paths
- rollout trust gates require artifact review and baseline comparison rather than standalone pass/fail output

## Important Notes
Phase 5 is the proof layer. It exists to make future maintainers confident that the learning system is not only sophisticated, but correct, performant, and operable.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-17T19:51:32.840968462Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-18T00:50:07.337671354Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","atc","atc-learning","atc-learning-phase5"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.5","depends_on_id":"br-0qt6e","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T19:51:32.840968462Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1246,"issue_id":"br-0qt6e.5","author":"codex","text":"Phase 5 should use remote-only heavy verification.  {"id":"br-0qt6e.5.1","title":"ATC Learning 5.1: synthetic scenario corpus and deterministic replay harness","description":"# ATC Learning 5.1: Synthetic scenario corpus and deterministic replay harness

## Why
The learning loop needs a reusable corpus of scenario traces that can be replayed deterministically to validate capture, resolution, adaptation behavior, preflight preview behavior, operator-facing behavior, and user-visible surfaces.

## Deliverables
- define a corpus of ATC-relevant scenarios: healthy liveness, silent agent, false probe, advisory success, advisory failure, conflict resolution, deliberate no-op, safe-to-ignore noise, suppressed unsafe action, counterfactual candidate-scoring situations, drift onset, rollback, restart recovery, degraded-learning safe mode, preview-invalidated-by-regime-change, weak-support preview refusal, predicted-versus-realized mismatch cases, spoofed-liveness attempts, duplicate/replayed event streams, coordinated reservation churn, strategically contaminated feedback cases, overlapping ATC interventions, concurrent operator control changes, natural recovery during an attribution window, and exogenous changes that intentionally confound attribution
- make those scenarios replayable without live LLMs or nondeterministic external services
- capture enough structure to test delayed outcomes, censoring logic, no-action semantics, preview freshness invalidation, fairness/burden concentration, contamination suspicion, attribution confidence/confoundedness, and user-facing summaries from the same scenario inputs
- define canonical scenario manifests with stable scenario IDs, ordered timeline checkpoints, seeds, workload shapes, expected attribution-eligibility frontiers, and expected state transitions so every harness is comparing against the same truth over time
- define expected outcomes for each scenario at multiple layers: experience rows, rollups, policy state, contamination-suspicion state, attribution-confidence/confoundedness state, preflight previews, robot/TUI summaries, audit artifacts, operator-control/explainability surfaces where relevant, and emitted logs where relevant
- record scenario IDs, seeds, workload shapes, preview/reference comparators, attribution-comparator notes, and expected artifact bundles so failures are immediately reproducible and diagnosable
- document how new scenarios should be added over time without weakening determinism or making performance suites incomparable

## Acceptance Criteria
- the corpus includes both acted and deliberately-non-acted paths, including cases where ATC suppresses itself for safety, distrusts or quarantines evidence for robustness reasons, refuses to certify a preview, invalidates an old preview because the evidence or regime changed, or explicitly declines to learn because causal attribution is too confounded
- each scenario has explicit expected outputs for correctness, operator usability, preview-versus-realized truthfulness, contamination handling, attribution honesty, and logging/artifact behavior
- scenario manifests are stable enough to compare outputs across unit, replay, E2E, and performance harnesses without inventing separate truth sets or fuzzy end-state-only checks
- the same corpus can feed unit, replay, E2E, and performance harnesses without inventing separate truth sets

## Important Notes
This corpus is the common truth set for unit, E2E, performance, and hardening passes. It should be rich enough to test correctness, robustness, attribution honesty, and operator usability +//! User-facing utility, remediation guidance, noise control, and safe
+//! defaults for ATC surfaces (br-0qt6e.4.6).
+//!
+//! Defines how ATC communicates with operators and agents: what to show,
+//! when to show it, how to frame actions vs. non-actions, and how to
+//! keep the surface calm, fair, and trustworthy.
+//!
+//! # Design Principle
+//!
+//! Even a mathematically strong ATC system will feel bad if it is noisy,
+//! opaque, hard to act on, or unable to say when the evidence is not
+//! trustworthy. This module optimizes for real operators and agents.
+//!
+//! # Surface State Taxonomy
+//!
+//! Every ATC surface element must communicate one of these states:
+//!
+//! ```text
+//! ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
+//! │  Observation        — "ATC noticed X. No action needed."    │
+//! │  Recommendation     — "ATC suggests Y. Here's the evidence."│
+//! │  Executed           — "ATC did Z. Here's why and what next."│
+//! │  Suppressed         — "ATC held back. Here's why."          │
+//! │  Fairness Throttle  — "Paused to avoid over-burdening."     │
+//! │  Deliberate No-Op   — "ATC chose inaction. Here's why."     │
+//! │  Distrust           — "Evidence quality too low to act on."  │
+//! │  Confounded         — "Outcome is real but credit is unclear"│
+//! │  Safe-to-Ignore     — "Nothing requires attention right now."│
+//! └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
+//! ```
+//!
+//! # Noise Control Philosophy
+//!
+//! The system should be informative without becoming spammy:
+//! - Default surfaces show only actionable items
+//! - Repeated identical messages are suppressed after the first
+//! - Safe-no-action states are silent unless the operator drills down
+//! - Escalation is visible but not alarming until action is needed
+//! - Evidence-trust problems are surfaced honestly, not hidden
+
+#![allow(clippy::doc_markdown)]
+
+use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
+
+// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+// Surface state taxonomy
+// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+
+/// The state of an ATC surface element as presented to the user.
+///
+/// Every ATC output must be classifiable into exactly one of these states.
+/// This prevents unexplained silence and makes the system's reasoning
+/// legible to operators.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
+pub enum SurfaceState {
+    /// ATC observed something noteworthy but no action is needed.
+    /// Example: "Agent X has been idle for 3 minutes (normal for this agent)."
+    Observation,
+    /// ATC recommends an action but has not taken it.
+    /// Example: "Consider probing Agent X — idle for 8 minutes."
+    Recommendation,
+    /// ATC executed an intervention.
+    /// Example: "Released reservations for Agent X (confirmed inactive)."
+    ExecutedIntervention,
+    /// ATC considered an action but held back due to safety/calibration.
+    /// Example: "Release withheld — calibration uncertain."
+    SuppressedIntervention,
+    /// ATC throttled action to avoid over-burdening a target.
+    /// Example: "Advisory suppressed — Agent X received 3 advisories this hour."
+    FairnessThrottle,
+    /// ATC deliberately chose inaction as the optimal response.
+    /// Example: "No action needed — Agent X's behavior is within normal bounds."
+    DeliberateNoOp,
+    /// ATC cannot trust the evidence enough to act on it.
+    /// Example: "Evidence quarantined — possible contamination detected."
+    EvidenceDistrust,
+    /// The outcome is real but the causal story is not clean.
+    /// Example: "Agent recovered, but 3 overlapping interventions make credit unclear."
+    AttributionConfounded,
+    /// Nothing requires attention. This is the default calm state.
+    /// Example: "All agents healthy. No actions pending."
+    SafeToIgnore,
+}
+
+impl SurfaceState {
+    /// Whether this state is actionable (requires operator attention).
+    #[must_use]
+    pub const fn is_actionable(self) -> bool {
+        matches!(
+            self,
+            Self::Recommendation | Self::ExecutedIntervention | Self::EvidenceDistrust
+        )
+    }
+
+    /// Whether this state should appear in default (non-verbose) views.
+    #[must_use]
+    pub const fn visible_by_default(self) -> bool {
+        matches!(
+            self,
+            Self::Recommendation
+                | Self::ExecutedIntervention
+                | Self::EvidenceDistrust
+                | Self::AttributionConfounded
+        )
+    }
+
+    /// Whether this state should be silent unless the operator drills down.
+    #[must_use]
+    pub const fn silent_by_default(self) -> bool {
+        matches!(
+            self,
+            Self::Observation | Self::DeliberateNoOp | Self::SafeToIgnore
+        )
+    }
+
+    /// Human-readable label for UI display.
+    #[must_use]
+    pub const fn label(self) -> &'static str {
+        match self {
+            Self::Observation => "Observed",
+            Self::Recommendation => "Recommended",
+            Self::ExecutedIntervention => "Executed",
+            Self::SuppressedIntervention => "Suppressed",
+            Self::FairnessThrottle => "Throttled",
+            Self::DeliberateNoOp => "No Action",
+            Self::EvidenceDistrust => "Low Trust",
+            Self::AttributionConfounded => "Confounded",
+            Self::SafeToIgnore => "All Clear",
+        }
+    }
+
+    /// Icon hint for TUI/robot rendering.
+    #[must_use]
+    pub const fn icon_hint(self) -> &'static str {
+        match self {
+            Self::Observation => "eye",
+            Self::Recommendation => "lightbulb",
+            Self::ExecutedIntervention => "bolt",
+            Self::SuppressedIntervention => "pause",
+            Self::FairnessThrottle => "scale",
+            Self::DeliberateNoOp => "check",
+            Self::EvidenceDistrust => "shield_alert",
+            Self::AttributionConfounded => "help",
+            Self::SafeToIgnore => "check_circle",
+        }
+    }
+}
+
+impl std::fmt::Display for SurfaceState {
+    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
+        write!(f, "{}", self.label())
+    }
+}
+
+// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+// Surface card: the atomic unit of user-facing ATC output
+// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+
+/// A surface card: the atomic unit of ATC output for operators.
+///
+/// Every ATC communication to the user (TUI toast, robot status line,
+/// audit log entry, system health indicator) should be derivable from
+/// a `SurfaceCard`.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct SurfaceCard {
+    /// The state classification.
+    pub state: SurfaceState,
+    /// One-line headline (max 80 chars).
+    pub headline: String,
+    /// What happened (evidence summary).
+    pub what_happened: String,
+    /// Why it happened (reasoning chain).
+    pub why: String,
+    /// How risky the situation or action is.
+    pub risk_assessment: RiskAssessment,
+    /// What the operator should do next.
+    pub next_action: NextAction,
+    /// Whether this can be safely ignored.
+    pub safe_to_ignore: bool,
+    /// Evidence trust level for this card.
+    pub evidence_trust: EvidenceTrustLevel,
+    /// Attribution clarity for this card.
+    pub attribution_clarity: AttributionClarity,
+    /// Target agent or cohort (if applicable).
+    pub target: Option<String>,
+    /// Severity for display ordering (0.0 = informational, 1.0 = critical).
+    pub severity: f64,
+    /// When this card was generated (microseconds).
+    pub generated_ts_micros: i64,
+    /// Time-to-live before the card auto-expires (microseconds).
+    pub ttl_micros: i64,
+}
+
+/// Risk assessment for a surface card.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
+pub enum RiskAssessment {
+    /// No risk to operators or agents.
+    None,
+    /// Low risk: informational only.
+    Low,
+    /// Medium risk: monitoring recommended.
+    Medium,
+    /// High risk: action may be needed.
+    High,
+    /// Critical: immediate attention required.
+    Critical,
+}
+
+impl std::fmt::Display for RiskAssessment {
+    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
+        match self {
+            Self::None => write!(f, "none"),
+            Self::Low => write!(f, "low"),
+            Self::Medium => write!(f, "medium"),
+            Self::High => write!(f, "high"),
+            Self::Critical => write!(f, "critical"),
+        }
+    }
+}
+
+/// What the operator should do next.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct NextAction {
+    /// Concise instruction (max 120 chars).
+    pub instruction: String,
+    /// Whether action is required or optional.
+    pub required: bool,
+    /// Suggested command or path (if applicable).
+    pub command_hint: Option<String>,
+}
+
+/// Evidence trust level communicated to the user.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
+pub enum EvidenceTrustLevel {
+    /// Evidence is clean and trustworthy.
+    High,
+    /// Evidence has some uncertainty but is usable.
+    Moderate,
+    /// Evidence quality is low — decisions should be cautious.
+    Low,
+    /// Evidence is quarantined — do not trust this yet.
+    Quarantined,
+}
+
+impl EvidenceTrustLevel {
+    /// User-facing explanation of the trust level.
+    #[must_use]
+    pub const fn explanation(self) -> &'static str {
+        match self {
+            Self::High => "Evidence is clean and reliable.",
+            Self::Moderate => "Some uncertainty in the evidence, but sufficient for action.",
+            Self::Low => "Evidence quality is low. Exercise caution with automated decisions.",
+            Self::Quarantined => "Evidence has been quarantined due to suspected contamination. Do not act on this alone.",
+        }
+    }
+}
+
+impl std::fmt::Display for EvidenceTrustLevel {
+    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
+        match self {
+            Self::High => write!(f, "high"),
+            Self::Moderate => write!(f, "moderate"),
+            Self::Low => write!(f, "low"),
+            Self::Quarantined => write!(f, "quarantined"),
+        }
+    }
+}
+
+/// Attribution clarity communicated to the user.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
+pub enum AttributionClarity {
+    /// Single clear cause. Full confidence in the causal story.
+    Clean,
+    /// Multiple causes but one is dominant. Mostly clear.
+    Dominant,
+    /// Multiple overlapping causes. Credit story is not clean.
+    Confounded,
+    /// Cannot determine cause. Outcome is real but attribution is unknown.
+    Unknown,
+}
+
+impl AttributionClarity {
+    /// User-facing explanation.
+    #[must_use]
+    pub const fn explanation(self) -> &'static str {
+        match self {
+            Self::Clean => "Clear single cause identified.",
+            Self::Dominant => "Multiple factors, but one primary cause identified.",
+            Self::Confounded => "Multiple overlapping interventions make the credit story unclear. The outcome is real, but ATC cannot confidently assign credit.",
+            Self::Unknown => "Cannot determine what caused this outcome.",
+        }
+    }
+}
+
+impl std::fmt::Display for AttributionClarity {
+    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
+        match self {
+            Self::Clean => write!(f, "clean"),
+            Self::Dominant => write!(f, "dominant"),
+            Self::Confounded => write!(f, "confounded"),
+            Self::Unknown => write!(f, "unknown"),
+        }
+    }
+}
+
+// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+// Noise control policy
+// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+
+/// Noise control policy for a specific surface type.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct NoisePolicy {
+    /// Surface type this policy applies to.
+    pub surface: &'static str,
+    /// Maximum emissions per agent per hour.
+    pub max_per_agent_per_hour: u32,
+    /// Maximum emissions globally per minute.
+    pub max_global_per_minute: u32,
+    /// Cooldown between identical messages (microseconds).
+    pub dedup_cooldown_micros: i64,
+    /// Whether to auto-suppress after repeated occurrences.
+    pub auto_suppress_after: u32,
+    /// Escalation threshold (after N suppressions, escalate).
+    pub escalation_threshold: u32,
+    /// Whether this surface type is interruptive (toast, alert).

+// Safe defaults
+// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+
+/// Safe defaults for surface behavior.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+pub struct SafeDefaults {
+    /// Default view mode (summary vs. drill-down).
+    pub default_view: ViewMode,
+    /// Whether interruptive surfaces (toasts, alerts) are enabled.
+    pub interruptive_enabled: bool,
+    /// Minimum severity for interruptive surfaces (0.0-1.0).
+    pub interruptive_min_severity: f64,
+    /// Whether to show attribution-confounded outcomes.
+    pub show_confounded: bool,
+    /// Whether to show evidence-distrust indicators.
+    pub show_distrust: bool,
+    /// Whether to show fairness throttle indicators.
+    pub show_fairness_throttle: bool,
+    /// Whether to show deliberate no-ops in the timeline.
+    pub show_no_ops: bool,
+    /// Default auto-dismiss time for info toasts (seconds).
+    pub toast_info_dismiss_secs: u32,
+    /// Default auto-dismiss time for warning toasts (seconds).
+    pub toast_warn_dismiss_secs: u32,
+    /// Default auto-dismiss time for error toasts (seconds).
+    pub toast_error_dismiss_secs: u32,
+}
+
+/// View mode for surface rendering.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]
+#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
+pub enum ViewMode {
+    /// Summary view: only actionable items, concise format.
+    Summary,
+    /// Detailed view: includes observations, no-ops, full evidence.
+    Detailed,
+    /// Forensic view: everything including raw metrics and internals.
+    Forensic,
+}
+
+impl std::fmt::Display for ViewMode {
+    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
+        match self {
+            Self::Summary => write!(f, "summary"),
+            Self::Detailed => write!(f, "detailed"),
+            Self::Forensic => write!(f, "forensic"),
+        }
+    }
+}
+
+/// The canonical safe defaults for ATC surfaces.
+pub const SAFE_DEFAULTS: SafeDefaults = SafeDefaults {
+    default_view: ViewMode::Summary,
+    interruptive_enabled: true,
+    interruptive_min_severity: 0.50, // only medium+ severity interrupts
+    show_confounded: true,           // operators should know when credit is unclear
+    show_distrust: true,             // operators should know when evidence is bad
+    show_fairness_throttle: true,    // operators should know when throttling happens
+    show_no_ops: false,              // no-ops are silent by default
+    toast_info_dismiss_secs: 5,
+    toast_warn_dismiss_secs: 8,
+    toast_error_dismiss_secs: 15,
+};

+    /// What should NOT happen.
+    pub anti_pattern: &'static str,
+}

+// Surface card builder
+// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+
+/// Build a surface card for the given state and context.
+#[must_use]
+pub fn build_surface_card(
+    state: SurfaceState,
+    headline: String,
+    what_happened: String,
+    why: String,
+    risk: RiskAssessment,
+    next_action_text: &str,
+    target: Option<String>,
+    now_micros: i64,
+) -> SurfaceCard {
+    let severity = match risk {
+        RiskAssessment::None => 0.0,
+        RiskAssessment::Low => 0.25,
+        RiskAssessment::Medium => 0.50,
+        RiskAssessment::High => 0.75,
+        RiskAssessment::Critical => 1.0,
+    };
+
+    let ttl = match state {
+        SurfaceState::SafeToIgnore | SurfaceState::DeliberateNoOp => 60_000_000, // 1 min
+        SurfaceState::Observation => 300_000_000,    // 5 min
+        SurfaceState::FairnessThrottle => 600_000_000, // 10 min
+        _ => 1_800_000_000, // 30 min for actionable items
+    };
+
+    SurfaceCard {
+        state,
+        headline,
+        what_happened,
+        why,
+        risk_assessment: risk,
+        next_action: NextAction {
+            instruction: next_action_text.to_string(),
+            required: state.is_actionable(),
+            command_hint: None,
+        },
+        safe_to_ignore: state.silent_by_default(),
+        evidence_trust: EvidenceTrustLevel::High,
+        attribution_clarity: AttributionClarity::Clean,
+        target,
+        severity,
+        generated_ts_micros: now_micros,
+        ttl_micros: ttl,
+    }
+}
